purpose: Maintains minimum system pressure by preventing pressure drops below safety threshold during depressurization events

inputs.name: system_pressure
inputs.description: Current system pressure upstream of valve

inputs.name: flow_demand
inputs.description: Downstream flow demand that could cause pressure drop

outputs.name: valve_position
outputs.description: Valve opening position from 0 (closed) to 1 (fully open)

states.name: valve_integrity
states.description: Valve sealing and mechanical integrity, degrades due to flow-induced wear

states.name: control_functionality
states.description: Control system ability to respond to pressure changes

states.name: tracked_pressure
states.description: Internal tracking of system pressure for event detection

events.name: minimum_pressure_valve_leaking
events.description: Valve allows pressure to drop below minimum threshold when net is depressurized

events.name: minimum_pressure_valve_malfunction
events.description: Valve fails to maintain minimum pressure causing safety valve activation

parameters.name: valve_degradation_timescale
parameters.description: Time constant for valve mechanical degradation

parameters.name: control_degradation_timescale
parameters.description: Time constant for control system degradation

parameters.name: leakage_threshold
parameters.description: Valve integrity threshold for leakage detection

parameters.name: malfunction_threshold
parameters.description: Control functionality threshold for malfunction detection

state_transition_logic: valve_integrity decreases by (1.0 / valve_degradation_timescale) * flow_demand * dt, clamped to [0.0, 1.0]
control_functionality decreases by (1.0 / control_degradation_timescale) * system_pressure * dt, clamped to [0.0, 1.0]
tracked_pressure approaches system_pressure using first-order lag with pressure_tracking_coefficient, clamped to [0.0, 1000000.0]
output_logic: valve_position = valve_response_coefficient * max(0, (minimum_pressure_threshold - system_pressure) / minimum_pressure_threshold) * control_functionality
assumptions: Valve degradation is primarily driven by flow-induced mechanical wear
Control system degradation correlates with pressure cycling frequency
Valve can only add pressure regulation, not reduce upstream pressure
Leakage reduces valve effectiveness proportionally to integrity loss
Control malfunction prevents proper pressure regulation response
